title: IoT Device Template Schema
description: Canonical schema for IoT device template manifests. backend/device-template-schema.json is the authoritative structural contract for custom template import, AI add_template, and default template resources; runtime then applies DTO validation, BoardStorageServiceImpl.validateTemplateManifestForNuSmv(), and a NuSMV probe pre-check for semantic constraints.

Name.description: Device template name (will be sanitized for NuSMV via sanitizeSmvToken: spaces removed, non-alphanumeric replaced with '_')

Icon.description: Optional self-contained template icon as a data:image URI. Remote image URLs are rejected so opening a template cannot contact an untrusted host. If omitted, the frontend uses a bundled default icon or generates a stable fallback icon.

Modes.description: Operating modes for multi-mode devices (e.g., ['ThermostatFanMode', 'ThermostatMode']). Because this schema validates raw manifests at the API/AI/default-template boundary, each mode must already be a valid NuSMV identifier before submission; use 'ThermostatMode' rather than 'Thermostat Mode'. Runtime NuSMV validation repeats normalization checks as a defensive semantic pass.

InitState.description: For a stateful template, the concrete initial configuration: single-mode 'stateName' or one semicolon-separated value per mode, exactly matching a WorkingStates.Name. Wildcards, partial tuples, and case-different aliases are rejected. A stateless template may omit this field or use an empty string because it has no device state to initialize.

WorkingStates.description: All possible working states of the device

Name.description: State name. For multi-mode: semicolon-separated (e.g., 'auto;cool'). Spaces are removed by sanitizeSmvToken (e.g., 'emergency heat' -> 'emergencyheat').

Dynamics.description: State-dependent variable effects. VariableName must be a local InternalVariable or a declared ImpactedVariable. Numeric domains require ChangeRate; enum/boolean domains require Value. One state may define each target at most once.

ChangeRate.description: Integer change rate per time unit (mutually exclusive with Value)

Value.description: Direct value assignment (mutually exclusive with ChangeRate)

Trust.description: MEDIC control-source trust label for this state component (normalized to lowercase). It records whether the state represents a trusted user-controlled origin when it contributes to an automation; it is not authentication, generic data integrity, or compromise probability.

Privacy.description: Data-sensitivity label for this state component (normalized to lowercase). It propagates through rules when privacy modeling is enabled; it is not access control or encryption.

InternalVariables.description: Device internal variables and environment variables

Name.description: Variable name (must be valid NuSMV identifier). Runtime validates against full NUSMV_RESERVED_WORDS set (case-insensitive).

IsInside.description: Required ownership choice: true = device-local value on this instance; false = scene-wide environment value shared across devices. Omission is rejected because silently choosing shared scope changes rule, import, and attack semantics.

FalsifiableWhenCompromised.description: Whether a compromised instance may report any value from this variable's declared domain and force its trust label to untrusted. Set true only for sensor readings or received data that compromise can falsify; actuator state, progress, and setpoints should normally be false.

Trust.description: MEDIC control-source trust label. If this value triggers a rule, one trusted contributing source retains a trusted target; only all-untrusted trigger sources make the target untrusted.

Values.description: Enumerated values (mutually exclusive with LowerBound/UpperBound). Spaces in values will be removed by sanitizeSmvToken (e.g., 'fan only' -> 'fanonly').

LowerBound.description: Minimum value for integer range (requires UpperBound)

UpperBound.description: Maximum accepted value for this variable. Attack modeling never widens the declared domain; a compromised sensor may choose any value within this range.

NaturalChangeRate.description: Natural change rate: single value '1' or range '[-1, 1]'

EnvironmentDomains.description: Domains/default labels for shared environment variables this template affects without necessarily reading. Each ImpactedVariables name must be defined here or by an IsInside=false InternalVariable in the same manifest.

Trust.description: MEDIC control-source trust label for this shared environment value; not authentication or generic integrity.

ImpactedVariables.description: List of environment variables this device can affect. Runtime validates as NuSMV identifiers (no spaces, no reserved words) via validateSmvIdentifier().

Transitions.description: Condition-triggered autonomous device behavior with one state or variable effect. Multi-effect actions are rejected because the current formal model cannot preserve them atomically. User-consumable event pulses belong to APIs with Signal=true.

Name.description: Transition name used for diagnostics and template display

StartState.description: Starting state. Empty string '' means 'from any state'. '_' is wildcard. For multi-mode: semicolon-separated partial patterns allowed (e.g., ';sendingAlertMessage').

EndState.description: Target state after transition. Optional when one variable assignment is used. For multi-mode templates, semicolon-separated partial tuples are allowed but at most one mode may have a concrete target in one Transition. State and variable effects cannot be combined in one Transition.

Trigger.description: Condition that triggers this transition. Required for non-signal transitions; validated by SmvModelValidator.validateTrigger().

Attribute.description: Variable or mode name to check

Relation.description: Comparison operator. EQ/NEQ/GT/GTE/LT/LTE/== are normalized to =/!=/>/>=/</<= by SmvRelationUtils.normalizeTriggerRelation().

Value.description: Value to compare against (spaces will be removed by generator)

Assignments.description: At most one internal/shared-variable assignment when the transition occurs. It requires a Trigger and cannot be combined with EndState, so accepted behavior remains one atomic modeled effect.

APIs.description: Device commands/actions that automation rules can invoke. They change declared device state; they are not network endpoint definitions.

Name.description: API name (spaces allowed). Signal variable name is generated via formatApiSignalName: non-alphanumeric chars (including spaces) replaced with '_', then appended with '_a'. E.g., 'take photo' -> signal variable 'take_photo_a'.

StartState.description: Required explicit command precondition. Use empty string '' (or '_' components) to deliberately allow any state; otherwise provide the concrete single/multi-mode start pattern. Omission is rejected because silently choosing any state broadens the modeled action.

EndState.description: Target state after API call. All 45 existing templates have non-empty EndState. For multi-mode: semicolon-separated. Partial changes like ';cool' or 'auto;' allowed.

Trigger.description: Always null for APIs (triggers are for Transitions only)

Assignments.description: Must be empty. API commands are modeled as state actions through StartState and EndState; variable assignments on APIs are not part of the formal semantics. Use a triggered Transition for internal or shared-variable assignments.

Signal.description: Required explicit choice. true means this state-changing action is observable as a one-step automation/specification trigger; false means it is command-only. Omission is rejected because silently choosing false changes which rules and specifications are representable.

AcceptsContent.description: Optional capability, default false. true means a rule invoking this action may propagate one explicitly selected Content sensitivity label to the target state. It does not copy payload bytes or imply access control, encryption, or transmission.

Contents.description: Content/data items managed by the device (e.g., photos, data). Used for privacy checking in specifications.

$comment: Validation Notes: 1) Empty StartState ('') is intentional for APIs callable from any state (22/29 API-bearing templates); 7 templates have non-empty API StartState. 2) Name sanitization differs by context: sanitizeSmvToken removes spaces then replaces non-alnum with '_'; formatApiSignalName replaces non-alnum (including spaces) with '_' then appends '_a'. 3) NuSMV reserved words are rejected case-insensitively by runtime validation. 4) Trust/Privacy values are normalized to lowercase. 5) Multi-mode states use semicolon separator (e.g., 'mode1;mode2'). InitState and WorkingStates are complete configurations; only API/Transition endpoints may use partial tuples. 6) Partial state changes use empty parts (e.g., ';cool' changes only mode2). 7) InternalVariable/ImpactedVariable names must not contain spaces (rejected at validation time). 8) ImpactedVariable may share a name only with an environment InternalVariable (IsInside=false), meaning the device can read and affect the same shared environment variable; it must not share a name with a local InternalVariable (IsInside=true). 9) Generator supports state relation operators = != in 'not in' for state attributes; > < >= <= only for internal variables. 10) Transition.EndState is optional: when absent, no state change occurs but triggered assignments (e.g., variable resets) still execute (see Clock.json). 11) Schema limits that are intentionally paired with runtime checks: (a) Reserved word checks are described in schema and enforced case-insensitively at runtime. (b) Modes must be legal identifiers in raw manifests; runtime repeats mode normalization/collision checks as a defensive semantic pass. (c) APIs.Trigger is constrained to null because API triggers have no generator semantics. (d) APIs.Assignments must be empty because API actions have state-transition semantics only. 12) Prefix-like names such as variable_, a_, trust_, and privacy_ are literal business names, not aliases; runtime rejects only concrete generated NuSMV identifier collisions such as api_a, var_rate, trust_var, privacy_var, and is_attack. 13) IsInside is explicit because variable ownership cannot be inferred safely; omission never defaults to shared scope. 14) FalsifiableWhenCompromised is explicit because API presence does not determine whether a variable is sensor data; attack modeling randomizes only variables marked true and never widens their declared domains. 15) Every InternalVariable has an explicit enum Values list or complete numeric bounds; boolean values use an explicit [TRUE, FALSE] enum rather than an omitted domain.
